question: A bus travels 1,200 miles from Boston, MA, to Miami, FL. The bus covers the first 360 miles in 6 hours. If the bus continues to travel at this rate, how many more hours will it take to reach Miami? Round your answer to the nearest whole hour.

solution.strategy: This question requires several steps, so proceed methodically. All calculations center around the basic formula d = rt.
steps: Find the rate at which the bus is traveling: 360 ÷ 6 = 60 miles per hour.
Find the remaining distance: 1,200 - 360 = 840 miles.
Find the time for the remaining distance: 840 ÷ 60 = 14 hours.

question: A bus travels 900 miles from Chicago, IL, to Denver, CO. The bus covers the first 270 miles in 5 hours. If the bus continues to travel at this rate, how many more hours will it take to reach Denver? Round your answer to the nearest whole hour.

solution.strategy: This question requires several steps, so proceed methodically. All calculations center around the basic formula d = rt.
steps: Find the rate at which the bus is traveling: 270 ÷ 5 = 54 miles per hour.
Find the remaining distance: 900 - 270 = 630 miles.
Find the time for the remaining distance: 630 ÷ 54 ≈ 11.67, round to 12 hours.

question: A bus travels 1,500 miles from Seattle, WA, to San Diego, CA. The bus covers the first 400 miles in 8 hours. If the bus continues to travel at this rate, how many more hours will it take to reach San Diego? Round your answer to the nearest whole hour.

Find the remaining distance: 1,500 - 400 = 1,100 miles.
Find the time for the remaining distance: 1,100 ÷ 50 = 22 hours.
